title: Build a REST API for User Management
description: Create a Flask/FastAPI REST API with CRUD operations for managing users. The API should support creating, reading, updating, and deleting users with proper validation, error handling, and HTTP status codes. Include pagination for the list endpoint.
starter_code:
from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import Optional

app = FastAPI()

# In-memory storage
users_db = {}

class UserCreate(BaseModel):
    name: str
    email: str
    age: Optional[int] = None

class UserUpdate(BaseModel):
    name: Optional[str] = None
    email: Optional[str] = None
    age: Optional[int] = None

# TODO: Implement the following endpoints
# POST /users - Create a user
# GET /users - List users (with pagination)
# GET /users/{user_id} - Get a single user
# PUT /users/{user_id} - Update a user
# DELETE /users/{user_id} - Delete a user

evaluation_rubric.code_quality: Clean, readable code with proper naming
evaluation_rubric.error_handling: Proper HTTP status codes and error messages

title: Implement an LRU Cache from Scratch
description: Implement a Least Recently Used (LRU) cache class with O(1) get and put operations. The cache should have a fixed capacity and evict the least recently used item when full. Do not use collections.OrderedDict.
starter_code:
class LRUCache:
    """
    Implement a Least Recently Used (LRU) Cache.
    
    Operations:
    - get(key) -> value or -1 if not found
    - put(key, value) -> None (evict LRU if at capacity)
    
    Both operations must be O(1).
    
    Example:
        cache = LRUCache(2)
        cache.put(1, 1)
        cache.put(2, 2)
        cache.get(1)      # returns 1
        cache.put(3, 3)   # evicts key 2
        cache.get(2)      # returns -1
    """

title: Review and Improve This Database Module
description: Review the following database connection module. Identify issues related to security, performance, error handling, and best practices. Provide a refactored version with explanations.
starter_code:
# Review and improve this database module
import sqlite3
import os

DB_PATH = "app.db"
PASSWORD = "admin123"  # Database access password

def get_connection():
    conn = sqlite3.connect(DB_PATH)
    return conn

def create_user(name, email, role):
    conn = get_connection()
    query = f"INSERT INTO users (name, email, role) VALUES ('{name}', '{email}', '{role}')"
    conn.execute(query)
    conn.commit()
    conn.close()

def get_user(user_id):
    conn = get_connection()
    result = conn.execute(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = {user_id}").fetchone()
    return result

def get_all_users():
    conn = get_connection()
    results = conn.execute("SELECT * FROM users").fetchall()
    return results

def delete_user(user_id):
    conn = get_connection()
    conn.execute(f"DELETE FROM users WHERE id = {user_id}")
    conn.commit()

def search_users(query):
    conn = get_connection()
    results = conn.execute(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE name LIKE '%{query}%'").fetchall()
    return results

test_cases.expected: Connection pooling or context managers used

evaluation_rubric.correctness: All issues identified, refactored code works
evaluation_rubric.error_handling: Comprehensive error handling in refactored version
evaluation_rubric.readability: Clear explanations of each issue and fix
